pages.title: Wood Stave
pages.text: Craft your first stave: plank in the top-right, stick in the bottom-left.$(br2)It is not a vertical vanilla-tool shape.

pages.title: Upgrade Layout
pages.text: Upgrade in a crafting table: advanced material in the top-right, old stave in the bottom-left.$(br2)Main path: Wood→Stone→Iron→Diamond→Netherite.$(br)Branches: Stone→Copper (endpoint), Iron→Gold (endpoint).$(br2)The next pages show the layout. If no result appears, first make sure the current stave has won its tribulation and is upgrade-ready.

pages.heading: Main Upgrades I
pages.note: Stone accepts vanilla stone tool materials; cobblestone is shown here.

pages.heading: Main Upgrades II
pages.note: Each old stave must win its tribulation before upgrading.

pages.heading: Branch Upgrades
pages.note: Copper and Gold are endpoint branches.

pages.title: Momentum & Tribulation
pages.text: Writing a valid glyph gives +1 momentum. Winning 入 grants bonus momentum: Wood+5, Stone+8, Copper+10, Iron/Gold+12, Diamond/Netherite+15.$(br2)When full, write $(l)劫$(), win the tribulation arena, and the current stave becomes upgrade-ready.

pages.title: Stage Targets
pages.text: Wood30 → Stone45 → Iron70 → Diamond110 → Netherite160.$(br2)Copper55 and Gold90 are branch targets. Branch staves cannot continue upgrading; craft a new main-path stave to continue.

pages.title: Repair
pages.text: Anvil: first slot = stave to keep, second slot = same-tier stave or $(l)Ink Core$().$(br)Ink Cores repair 250 durability each, max 4 per repair.$(br)Same-tier repair keeps the first stave's data.$(br2)Higher tiers cost more XP and cores.
